choices.nok: You sneak perfectly until a chunk of wall Mongo broke falls in front of you. You dive aside — right into a guard who was running FROM Mongo. You both look at each other, scream, and run in opposite directions. You reach the throne room, but not elegantly.

godmother_apparition.title: The Fairy Godmother's Echo
godmother_apparition.text:
Deep in the potion factory, a cauldron you didn't touch begins to GLOW. Sparkles spiral upward, forming a shimmering apparition — the Fairy Godmother herself, or rather, her magical echo, imprinted on the lab.

"Oh my, oh my!" The echo adjusts her spectral glasses. "Visitors in my lab! And you've been touching my THINGS!"

She flutters around the equipment. "I see what my SON has been up to. Loyalty potions in the WATER SUPPLY? That's MY recipe but it's all WRONG. He added too much vanity extract — amateur!"

She turns to you. "I may be an echo of my former self, but I still have POWER in this lab. I can help you stop him. A mother's love doesn't mean a mother's BLINDNESS."

Her wand-echo sparks. "What do you need, dearie?"

choices.text: ✨ Ask her to brew the ULTIMATE counter-potion
choices.ok: Together you brew a potion that doesn't just counter loyalty — it reveals the TRUE feelings of everyone who drinks it. "When this hits the water supply, everyone will see Charming for who he REALLY is." She adds a sparkle for flair. "Presentation matters, dearie."
choices.nok: The potion is ALMOST right but the Fairy Godmother's echo flickers at the crucial moment. It'll work... probably. "70/30 chance," she says. "Those were MY odds when I started this business!" Not the most reassuring quality control.

choices.text: 🗣️ Convince her to haunt Charming at the ball
choices.ok: "Appear at the ball? In front of EVERYONE?" She considers. "Well... I DO love a dramatic entrance. And that boy needs a TALKING TO." She agrees to manifest at the critical moment. Having a ghostly Fairy Godmother as backup? That's an ace up your sleeve.
choices.nok: "I can't maintain my form outside this lab for long, dearie. Too much magic required." She'll try, but no guarantees. She gives you a charm that might summon her for thirty seconds. Better than nothing. Probably.

choices.text: 🗡️ Take her most powerful wand from the vault
choices.ok: She opens her personal vault — behind a shelf of "Happily Ever After" potions. Inside: her REAL wand. "The one Charming has is a COPY. This is the original." It hums with power. "Use it wisely, dearie. And tell my son... he's GROUNDED."
choices.nok: The vault is booby-trapped even for the Fairy Godmother's echo. A sleeping spell knocks you out for five minutes. You wake up with perfect hair. "Side effect," she shrugs. You take a lesser wand — powerful, but not THE wand.

piper_showdown.title: The Pied Piper's Duel
piper_showdown.text:
Between the swamp and the rally point, the Pied Piper blocks your path. He stands on a hill, silhouetted against the sky, flute raised.

"Going somewhere?" He plays a single haunting note. Your feet twitch.

"Rumpelstiltskin sends his regards. You've been causing TROUBLE." Another note. Donkey starts tap-dancing involuntarily. "I CAN'T STOP MY HOOVES!"

The Piper's music is a weapon — it compels anyone who hears it to dance, march, or freeze. His army doesn't need swords. Just rhythm.

"Dance for me," he croons, launching into a melody. Your legs want to MOVE.

Shrek jams swamp mud in his ears. "Can't hear what I can't hear!" Smart ogre.

choices.text: 💪 Plug ears and CHARGE — brute force beats melody
choices.ok: Mud in ears, fury in heart. You charge uphill while the Piper plays frantically. His music can't grip you if you can't HEAR it. One solid hit sends his flute flying. "MY FLUTE!" Without it, he's just a weird guy in colorful pants. "I SURRENDER!" Smart choice.
choices.nok: The mud falls out halfway up the hill. The music hits and you break into an involuntary jig. You're tap-dancing TOWARD him, which is technically still charging. You headbutt him mid-pirouette. Not graceful, but effective. The flute cracks.

choices.text: 🎵 Counter his music with Donkey's singing
choices.ok:
"DONKEY! SING! AS LOUD AS YOU CAN!" Donkey belts out "I'm a Believer" at maximum volume. It's AWFUL. It's BEAUTIFUL. It completely drowns out the Piper's flute. His spell breaks. Everyone stops dancing.

The Piper covers his ears. "MAKE IT STOP!" Now HE knows how it feels. Donkey takes a bow.
choices.nok: Donkey sings but the Piper adapts, harmonizing. Now they're in a DUET. Everyone is dancing even HARDER. "This isn't what I meant!" You tackle the Piper mid-verse and the music stops. Donkey keeps singing for another three minutes because he's having fun.

choices.text: ✨ Cast silence — no sound, no power
choices.ok: You cast a zone of magical silence. The Piper plays and plays — but NO SOUND comes out. His face goes from confident to confused to PANICKED. Without music, he's powerless. You walk up calmly, take his flute, and snap it. Silence is golden.
choices.nok: Your silence spell is patchy — works in spots, fails in others. The Piper plays a game of cat-and-mouse, darting between zones. You chase him through patches of sound and silence like the world's most annoying game of tag. Eventually Shrek clotheslines him.

donkey_dragon_reunion.title: Donkey Finds Dragon
donkey_dragon_reunion.text:
In this twisted timeline, Dragon is Rumpelstiltskin's prisoner — chained in a volcanic pit, forced to power his fortress furnaces with her fire breath.

Donkey sees her and FREEZES. Then his eyes fill with tears.

"Baby? BABY! What did they DO to you?!"

Dragon lifts her head. She doesn't recognize this timeline's Donkey — they never met here. She SNARLS, chains rattling.

"She doesn't know me," Donkey whispers, heartbroken. "In this world... we never..."

Fiona puts a hand on his shoulder. "Love transcends timelines, Donkey. If your bond is real, she'll feel it."

Donkey walks toward the snarling dragon. No fear. Just love. "Hey girl. I know you don't remember me. But I remember YOU."

choices.text: 🗣️ Help Donkey reach Dragon with words of love
choices.ok:
Donkey talks to Dragon. Not his usual chatter — real, quiet words. About their kids. About flying together. About the first time she didn't eat him. Dragon's eyes soften. A rumble — not a growl. A PURR. She lowers her head to Donkey. The chains glow and SHATTER.

"Love is the most powerful magic," Fiona says, wiping her eyes. Shrek pretends he's not crying. He is.
choices.nok: Dragon snaps at Donkey but he doesn't flinch. "I ain't scared of you, baby. I never was." She hesitates. The bond isn't fully there, but she stops attacking. Not quite a reunion — but a truce. Donkey sits beside her, talking softly, while you work on the chains.

choices.text: 💪 Break Dragon's chains by force
choices.ok: While Donkey distracts Dragon with his heartfelt speech, you attack the chains. Enchanted iron — it takes everything you've got. CLANG. CLANG. CRACK! The chains shatter. Dragon ROARS in freedom. She looks at Donkey — really LOOKS — and something clicks. She remembers. Somehow, she REMEMBERS.
choices.nok: The chains are magical and fight back — they shock you with each strike. Your hands burn. But you keep hitting. The chains crack but don't break fully. Dragon, moved by the effort, blasts them with her own fire. THAT does it. Freedom tastes like fire and singed eyebrows.

choices.text: ✨ Dispel the enchantment on Dragon's chains
choices.ok:
Rumpelstiltskin's chains are bound by contract magic — but you've been studying his weaknesses. You find the magical clause and VOID it. The chains evaporate. Dragon stretches her wings for the first time in years. She looks at Donkey. Donkey looks at her.

"Hi," he says.

She scoops him up gently. Some things don't need timelines to be true.
choices.nok: The dispel partially works — one chain breaks, the others hold. Dragon helps by breathing fire on the weakened links. Together you free her, though the process is loud and attracts witch patrols. You'll need to move FAST to the resistance base.

cookie_war_kitchen.title: Cookie's War Kitchen
cookie_war_kitchen.text:
The resistance base hums with activity, but the REAL command center is Cookie's kitchen. The ogre chef stands over a massive cauldron, stirring something that smells like swamp gas and victory.

"Welcome to Cookie's War Kitchen!" He waves a battle-scarred ladle. "Where every recipe is a WEAPON!"

The shelves are lined with jars: "Exploding Onion Soup," "Flash-Bang Chili," "Smokescreen Stew," and a mysterious bottle labeled "DO NOT OPEN UNTIL FORTRESS."

"Rumpelstiltskin's fortress has magical defenses," Cookie explains. "But magic can't stop CHEMISTRY. My ogre-brew grenades can blast through enchanted walls. Problem is — I need a taste-tester. Last guy grew a third ear."

Shrek volunteers immediately. "Ogre stomach. Cast iron."

"I need more than taste-testing. I need help with the SPECIAL batch."

choices.text: ✨ Infuse the ogre-brew with magic for extra potency
choices.ok: You channel magic into Cookie's brew. It GLOWS. It BUBBLES. It briefly achieves sentience and says "DESTROY" before settling down. "PERFECT!" Cookie bottles it carefully. "These will blow through Rumpelstiltskin's wards like tissue paper." You've just created magical napalm. Ogre-style.
choices.nok: The magical infusion makes the brew unstable. One jar explodes, turning the kitchen wall into cheese. "That's... new," Cookie says. The remaining jars are powerful but unpredictable. Could be explosions. Could be cheese. War is full of surprises.

choices.text: 💪 Carry the heavy supply crates to the front line
choices.ok: Cookie's brew is HEAVY — enchanted cast-iron jars. You haul crates that would take three normal people. "THAT'S what I like to see!" Cookie slaps your back (it hurts). The front line troops cheer when you arrive fully stocked. Ammunition: served.
choices.nok: You trip on a root and a crate breaks open. Three jars shatter. The explosion turns a patch of swamp into a crater. "CAREFUL!" Cookie yells. "That was my GOOD batch!" You deliver the rest more carefully, minus a few supplies.

choices.text: 🗣️ Boost the troops' morale with a feast before the assault
choices.ok: "Before we fight — we EAT!" Cookie's feast is legendary. Swamp-rat stew, onion bread, mud pie (actual pie, not actual mud — mostly). The resistance eats, laughs, shares stories. By the time you march on the fortress, the army's spirit is UNBREAKABLE. Full stomachs, full hearts.
choices.nok: The feast is great until Donkey eats something that disagrees with him. Loudly. The resulting... atmosphere... clears three tents. Cookie is offended. "My stew is PERFECT! His stomach is WEAK!" Morale is mixed. But at least everyone bonded over the experience.

fiona_war_council.title: Fiona's War Council
fiona_war_council.text:
In a torchlit cave, Fiona stands over a sand-table map of Rumpelstiltskin's fortress. She's in full warrior mode — armor, war paint, and an expression that could curdle milk.

"Listen up. We get ONE shot at this." She moves sand figures across the map. "Rumple's fortress has three layers: outer wall, inner keep, and the tower where the spinning wheel sits. Each layer has magical defenses."

Shrek stands beside her, arms crossed. Donkey is surprisingly focused. Puss in Boots sharpens his sword. Dragon circles overhead.

"We need to decide our approach. The full army can assault the walls while a strike team breaches the tower. Or we can be surgical about it."

She looks at you. "You've gotten us this far. What's the plan, General?"

choices.text: ⚔️ Full army assault — break through all three layers
choices.ok: "We hit them with EVERYTHING." Fiona grins. "I was hoping you'd say that." She assigns roles: Dragon breaks the outer wall, the ogres storm the keep, and the strike team races for the tower. "This is what we've been fighting for. Let's END this." The army roars.
choices.nok: The full assault plan has gaps — Fiona spots a weakness in the left flank. "If Rumple's witches hit here, we crumble." She adjusts the plan on the fly, but the delay costs you the element of surprise. Scouts report Rumple is reinforcing his walls.

choices.text: 🌙 Surgical strike — small team, maximum stealth
choices.ok: "Five of us. You, me, Shrek, Puss, and Donkey." Fiona nods. "Small, fast, deadly. The army distracts while we slip in." She traces a path through the sewers. "Nobody expects heroes to crawl through sewage." Shrek shrugs. "I live in a swamp. This is Tuesday."
choices.nok: The stealth plan is solid but Dragon can't be stealthy. She's the size of a house. Fiona adapts: Dragon joins the distraction force while you infiltrate. Less firepower, but more flexibility. "Plan B is still a plan," Fiona says.

choices.text: 🗣️ Diplomacy first — send terms, then attack when Rumple refuses
choices.ok: "Offer terms. He refuses. Then we attack while he's laughing at our 'weakness.'" Fiona raises an eyebrow. "Using his arrogance as a weapon. I like it." You send a surrender demand. Rumple laughs for TEN MINUTES. Meanwhile, your forces move into perfect position. His laughter dies when he sees the army at his gates.
choices.nok: Rumpelstiltskin responds to your terms with a counter-offer: "Surrender and I'll let you keep your SHOES." The insult fires up the troops better than any speech. "Well THAT backfired on him," Fiona smirks. Not the plan you intended, but the result works.

game_over.title: Game Over
game_over.text:
Your adventure ends here. The swamp grows quiet. The fairy tale goes untold.

But every story deserves another chance...

"Get up!" Donkey's voice echoes. "You can't quit NOW! We haven't even gotten to the good part!"

Shrek sighs. "...he's right. Annoyingly. Try again?"

ending_hero.title: Hero's Triumph
ending_hero.text:
The villain falls. The day is saved. Far Far Away celebrates with the biggest party the realm has ever seen.

Shrek tries to sneak away but Fiona catches him. "You're dancing. ONE dance." He grumbles but secretly smiles.

Donkey gives a toast that goes on for forty-five minutes. Nobody has the heart to stop him.

Puss in Boots writes a ballad about the adventure. It's mostly about himself, but he dedicates it to you.

The Magic Mirror shows the kingdom at peace. "And they all lived happily ever after," it says. Then adds: "Except Prince Charming. He's doing community service. In the swamp."

🏆 TRUE HERO ENDING 🏆
You faced evil head-on and WON through courage and strength.
The bards will sing of this day. Donkey will sing louder.

ending_dark.title: The Dark Ending
ending_dark.text:
Darkness falls across the land. The villain's power is absolute.

Shrek retreats to his swamp, which is all that remains uncorrupted — a tiny green refuge in a world gone wrong. Donkey won't stop pacing. Fiona sharpens her sword, planning the next attempt.

"We lost the battle," Fiona says quietly. "Not the war."

Shrek puts a hand on your shoulder. "Get some rest. We try again tomorrow. And the day after that. And the day after that."

Donkey adds: "Yeah! Because that's what heroes DO! They fail and then they try AGAIN! Like my singing career!"

Everyone winces. But he's right.

💀 DARK ENDING 💀
The villain won this round. But fairy tales don't end in darkness forever.
Somewhere, a new hero is waking up in a swamp...

ending_royal.title: Royal Ascension
ending_royal.text:
The rightful rulers are restored — and they've added a new title to the royal court: YOU.

Queen Fiona rules with wisdom and the occasional ogre roar. King Harold, finally un-frogged, learns to play golf. Queen Lillian starts a book club that's actually a secret intelligence network.

You serve as Royal Champion, which mostly means attending banquets and preventing Donkey from eating the decorations.

Prince Charming is sentenced to being the kingdom's hairdresser. He's actually excellent at it. Finds his true calling.

Puss in Boots becomes Captain of the Royal Guard. His first decree: mandatory nap time.

👑 ROYAL ENDING 👑
You didn't just save the kingdom — you became part of it.
Far Far Away has never been more far far fabulous.

ending_dragon.title: Dragon Rider
ending_dragon.text:
You and Dragon soar above the clouds, the corruption destroyed, the forest healing below. The bond between you is unbreakable — forged in fire and trust.

Donkey watches from below, tears streaming. "That's my BABY! And my FRIEND! Up there TOGETHER!" He sobs happy tears into Shrek's vest. Shrek pretends to be annoyed but pats his head.

Dragon takes you to her secret nest where the Dronkeys — her and Donkey's children — play among gems. They're equal parts adorable and terrifying.

You become the first Dragon Rider of Far Far Away, protecting the realm from the sky. The fairy tale creatures sleep soundly knowing a dragon watches over them.

🐉 DRAGON RIDER ENDING 🐉
Some heroes fight with swords. Some fight with magic.
You chose friendship — and got wings.

ending_trickster.title: Master of Tricks
ending_trickster.text:
You out-tricked the trickster, out-charmed the charmer, and out-smarted everyone in the room. The villain didn't just lose — they lost to their OWN game.

Word spreads across the land: there's someone cleverer than Rumpelstiltskin, wittier than Puss in Boots, and sneakier than the Big Bad Wolf.

Gingerbread Man offers you a job. "I'm starting a consulting firm. 'Fairy Tale Solutions Inc.' You'd be PERFECT."

You decline. There are more adventures out there. More tricks to play. More villains who think they're the smartest in the room.

Shrek watches you leave. "That one's trouble," he tells Fiona.
"The BEST kind of trouble," she replies.

🎭 TRICKSTER ENDING 🎭
Brains beat brawn. Wit beats wands.
You played the game and rewrote the rules.

ending_peace.title: Swamp Sweet Swamp
ending_peace.text:
The conflict ends not with a bang, but with a handshake. An uneasy peace — but peace nonetheless. The villain's power is broken enough to prevent tyranny, and the realm settles into a new normal.

You return to the swamp. Shrek makes onion soup. Fiona sets up a defensive perimeter (old habits). Donkey talks about his feelings for two hours straight.

It's not a grand victory. There's no parade. No crown. No dragon ride.

But Shrek hands you a bowl of soup and says, "You're alright. For a non-ogre."

From Shrek, that's basically a medal.

The sun sets over the swamp. It smells terrible. It's perfect.

💚 PEACE ENDING 💚
Not every hero needs glory. Sometimes the bravest thing
is choosing peace — and a really good bowl of onion soup.
